dialogos.texto_en: Hmm, matching souvenirs, eh?\nWhat kind of thing would be good?
With so many choices, it's difficult to decide.
Yo, what are you looking at?
Oh, it's Futaro-kun.
Before we go to the store, we were thinking of deciding\non the guidelines for searching for souvenirs.
I wonder if it would be best to get some clothes.\nI'd like to carry them around with me if possible.
Then how about a firefly glass accessory?
Firefly glass? Do you mean fluorite?
No, they're bluish dragonfly beads\nmade to resemble the sea in Okinawa.
There was a store over there\nwith lots of options for necklaces and earrings,\nand they looked good.
Oh, that's nice.\nIf it's blue, it would look good on Fu-kun too.
Wait, what about me wearing an accessory?
That's not strange at all.\nHow about a bracelet?
Well, if it's a bracelet, then...maybe?
Don't look so worried.\nI'll choose one that suits you.
O-okay.
The one I'm thinking of is around 20,000 yen,\nso it could be a good pick.
Well, if it's around that price,\nthen it's okay.
(Wow, that's expensive!\n I can't let myself be influenced\n by their sense of money!)
What else did you see that looked good?\nDid you come across anything else?
I stumbled upon some famous dyed fabric.\nWhat was it called again?
Was it 'bingata-zome'?\nI don't know much about it,\nbut I heard it's a traditional Okinawan product.
Yes, that's it!
Dyeing, eh.\nIt seems like an easy option for small items.\nI wonder if they also have kimonos?
We should go to a craftsman in the area\nand consider ordering a custom-made one.
(A-another suggestion that's likely to be expensive.\n My wallet won't be able to handle it for sure.)
C-custom-made might take a while.\nShould we consider something else?
Oh, right.\nMaking seven of them\nwill definitely take a long time.
Hmm, this is harder than I thought.
So, Futaro-kun, what do you think would be good?
That's right. We haven't heard your opinion yet,\nFu-kun.
Mine?\nLet's see...
(If I just leave them to decide on their own,\n who knows what kind of luxurious item\n they might choose. Now is the time to act!)
Instead of deciding what to get,\nlet's consider the budget first!
The budget?
Ah...
Oh, that's right. The budget is important.\nYeah, I think so too.
Hey, I guess you could call it having a sound\nfinancial sense. Even choosing a souvenir\nputs your everyday mindset to the test, you know?
...What's up?\nWhy are you suddenly acting weird?
Oh, I just remembered something important!\nYou have to keep a tight hold on your wallet.\nMaybe even keep track of your finances if you're on a budget.
Although you're making good money.
Well, it's not too bad, you know?
But, that's all the more reason\nto manage the money you earn, right?\nLike you said, having a good sense of money is important.
Of course.\nI always save a good portion of my paycheck.
Good to hear.
...What are we even talking about?
You're acting different today.\nYou woke up earlier than usual,\nand you're saying strange things.
Yeah, having a sense of money is important.\nYou guys won't be relying on your dad forever.
D-does that mean...
What is it?
N-nothing, never mind!
I was just imagining what it would be like\nto stand on my own two feet.\nAha, ahaha...
Well, even if I said standing on my own two feet,\nwhat I was imagining was...being with you...
You're acting different today.\nYou woke up earlier than usual,\nand you're saying strange things.
You really get it, Ichika. Back when you were\nliving in an apartment, you were the one\nmanaging the household budget, right?
Yeah, well, I guess.\nIt was tough staring at numbers every day.
W-well, thanks to that,\nI feel like I've become good at that kind of thing,\nyou know?
J-just kidding!
Hold on, hold on, you're no match for Raiha\nwhen it comes to managing finances.\nAfter all, she's got much more experience than you.
O-oh, I see.\nI guess I can't compete with Raiha-chan after all.
That's why I need to earn enough money\nso I can make things easier for Raiha...!
...What's with this dialogue?
You're acting different today.\nYou woke up earlier than usual,\nand you're saying strange things.
Huh? What's up, Nino?\nIs there something on my face?
No, it's nothing.\nLet's stop chatting and get back to our souvenirs search, 'kay?
Ah, okay.
Thanks to Futaro-kun,\nwe have a budget to work with now,\nso let's look for something affordable and nice.
Yeah, let's do that.
